name: CPU Profiling with --prof and clinic.js
description: Profile Node.js CPU usage to find performance bottlenecks using built-in V8 profiler and clinic.js flame charts.

steps.title: Profile with the built-in V8 profiler
steps.description: Start Node.js with the --prof flag to generate a V8 CPU profile log file.
steps.tip: Run your workload against the app while profiling. The log is written when the process exits.

steps.title: Process the V8 profile log
steps.description: Convert the raw V8 log into a human-readable summary showing where CPU time was spent.

steps.title: Review the profile results
steps.description: Examine the processed profile to identify hot functions and optimization opportunities.
steps.command:
# Look at the 'Summary' section first:
head -50 profile.txt

# Then check 'Bottom up (heavy) profile' for hot functions:
grep -A 30 'Bottom up' profile.txt | head -40
steps.tip: Focus on the 'ticks' column. Functions with the most ticks are consuming the most CPU time.

steps.title: Use clinic.js flame for visual profiling
steps.description: Generate an interactive flame chart to visually identify CPU bottlenecks in your application.
steps.tip: Wider bars in the flame chart mean more CPU time. Look for unexpectedly wide bars in your own code (not Node internals).

steps.title: Profile with Chrome DevTools Performance tab
steps.description: Use the DevTools Performance tab for interactive CPU profiling with timeline and call tree views.
steps.command:
node --inspect app.js
# In Chrome DevTools:
# 1. Go to Performance tab
# 2. Click Record
# 3. Run your workload
# 4. Stop recording and analyze

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Node.js CPU Profiling Script

APP_FILE="${1:-app.js}"
DURATION="${2:-30}"

echo "==> Profiling ${APP_FILE} for ${DURATION} seconds..."

# Clean up old profiles
rm -f isolate-*.log profile.txt

echo "==> Method 1: V8 --prof profiling..."
timeout ${DURATION} node --prof "${APP_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true

if ls isolate-*.log 1>/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "==> Processing V8 profile log..."
  node --prof-process isolate-*.log > profile.txt
  echo ""
  echo "==> Profile Summary:"
  head -30 profile.txt
  echo ""
  echo "Full profile saved to: profile.txt"
else
  echo "No V8 profile log generated."
fi

echo ""
echo "==> Method 2: clinic.js (if installed)..."
if command -v clinic &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Run: clinic flame -- node ${APP_FILE}"
  echo "Run: clinic doctor -- node ${APP_FILE}"
else
  echo "clinic.js not installed. Install with: npm install -g clinic"
fi

echo ""
echo "==> Method 3: Chrome DevTools profiling..."
echo "Run: node --inspect ${APP_FILE}"
echo "Open: chrome://inspect -> Performance tab -> Record"

faq.question: What is the difference between --prof and --inspect profiling?
faq.answer: The --prof flag generates a low-overhead statistical profile suitable for production-like environments. Chrome DevTools profiling via --inspect provides more detail and interactivity but has higher overhead.

faq.question: How do I profile a production server without downtime?
faq.answer: Use the --inspect flag and attach remotely, or use process._debugProcess(pid) to enable debugging on a running process. Alternatively, use 0x or clinic.js with a canary instance.
